title: Close the remaining screen-core video upside-down bug on the authored front-screen overlay path
summary: Finish the current screen-core presentation recovery by removing the last video-only orientation defect. The screen was already front-facing and horizontally readable after 0.2.5, but videos still inherited an older RenderTexture vertical flip that no longer belongs on the authored direct-CUA front-screen path.
reasoning: The operator retest after 0.2.5 narrowed the remaining problem further: still images were correct, but videos were upside down. That means the horizontal contract is now right and the remaining defect is specifically the VideoPlayer or RenderTexture Y-flip seam. Live code showed that ShouldFlipProjectedOverlayVertically still applied to every RenderTexture or VideoPlayer overlay, including the authored screen-core front path. Version 0.2.6 keeps the fix narrow by turning that legacy Y-flip off only for the direct-CUA screen-core surface, while preserving the older disconnect-surface and fallback overlay contracts separately.

changes.summary: Scoped the RenderTexture and VideoPlayer vertical-flip rule off for the authored screen-core front-screen overlay path.
changes.reasoning: The current direct-CUA screen-core front path now presents on the correct side and with the correct horizontal orientation. Carrying the older fallback Y-flip forward into that seam leaves videos readable only upside down.

changes.summary: Kept the vertical-flip correction on the older fallback and disconnect-surface overlay seams instead of removing it globally.
changes.reasoning: The recovery goal is to stabilize the current phase-1 direct-CUA screen lane without reopening older fallback paths that may still rely on the existing VideoPlayer texture contract.

verification: Stamp version 0.2.6 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs.
Build and deploy fa_cua_player.0.2.6.dll and fa_player_asset.0.2.6.assetbundle.
Validate the 0.2.6 release packet, including versioned live outputs and generated changelog artifacts.
knownIssues: Loop behavior still needs a dedicated follow-up if the operator continues to see single-style continuation under some loop settings.
Joystick and gaze focus remain intentionally deferred until the screen lane is fully stable.
